content.problemStatement:
Chef had a sequence of NNN **distinct** points X1,X2,...,XNX_1, X_2, ..., X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,...,XN‚Äč.

Chef did the following action to get a new sequence of points YYY:


Choose a pivot index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), and thus a pivot value P=XiP = X_iP=Xi‚Äč.
Make a list LLL of the NNN pairs (‚ą£Xj‚ąíP‚ą£,Xj)(|X_j - P|, X_j)(‚ą£Xj‚Äč‚ąíP‚ą£,Xj‚Äč) for each 1‚Č§j‚Č§N1 \le j \le N1‚Č§j‚Č§N, i.e. pair of (distance, value).
Sort the list LLL in increasing order‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä†.
Define YkY_kYk‚Äč as the **second** element in the kkk-th pair in this sorted list LLL.
For example,


Suppose we start with X=[2,6,5,4]X = [2, 6, 5, 4]X=[2,6,5,4], pivot index i=3i = 3i=3 and thus, P=X3=5P = X_3 = 5P=X3‚Äč=5.
List LLL = [(3,2),(1,6),(0,5),(1,4)][(3, 2), (1, 6), (0, 5), (1, 4)][(3,2),(1,6),(0,5),(1,4)]
Sorted order of List LLL = [(0,5),(1,4),(1,6),(3,2)][(0, 5), (1, 4), (1, 6), (3, 2)][(0,5),(1,4),(1,6),(3,2)]
Y=[5,4,6,2]Y = [5, 4, 6, 2]Y=[5,4,6,2] taking the second elements from the list.
Now, you are given the sequences XXX and YYY but you do not know the pivot index chosen by Chef. Can you recover **any valid** pivot index, or declare an inconsistency in the results. (i.e. no valid pivot index exists)

It is guaranteed the input values of the array XXX and YYY are all **distinct**, and that XXX is a permutation of YYY.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† Increasing order of pairs is defined as (A,B)<(C,D)(A, B) < (C, D)(A,B)<(C,D) if and only if **either** of the 222 conditions hold:


A<CA < CA<C
A=C,B<DA = C, B < DA=C,B<D

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of points.
The second line contains NNN integers - X1,X2,...,XNX_1, X_2, ..., X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,...,XN‚Äč, the initial sequence of **distinct** points.
The third line contains NNN integers - Y1,Y2,...,YNY_1, Y_2, ..., Y_NY1‚Äč,Y2‚Äč,...,YN‚Äč, the final sequence of **distinct** points.




Output Format
For each test case, output either the pivot index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N) if it exists or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 to indicate no valid pivot.


If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: Xi‚Č†XjX_i \ne X_jXi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Xj‚Äč for all i‚Č†ji \ne jiÓÄ†=j
Yi‚Č†YjY_i \ne Y_jYi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Yj‚Äč for all i‚Č†ji \ne jiÓÄ†=j
XXX is a permutation of YYY

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : As already discussed in the statement, we can choose pivot index i=3i = 3i=3.
Test Case 2 : The given order in YYY is incorrect, as 666 was placed before 444 if we choose pivot index i=3i = 3i=3 for example.
